properties.summary: Implemented three-tab navigation bar for BuildDetail page with: (1) _activeTab state variable initialized to 1, (2) ChangeTab method that updates state and calls StateHasChanged, (3) Three tab buttons (Build Details, AgentField, Logs) with tab_active CSS class on active tab, (4) Conditional rendering of tab content for each tab, (5) PageTitle truncated to 40 chars, (6) Truncate helper method added, (7) showBuildLogs and showContainerLogs booleans for logs toggle in Tab 3
properties.test_summary: Unit tests written for tab navigation functionality covering AC1 (3 tab buttons), AC2 (_activeTab state with default 1), AC3 (ChangeTab calls StateHasChanged), AC5 (only one tab active at a time). Tests cannot be executed without dotnet test runner available.
codebase_learnings: Uses Blazor Server model with Razor components (.razor files)
State variables are private fields in code block with camelCase naming (_activeTab, _loading)
Uses reflection in tests to access private fields for unit testing
PageTitle supports Truncate helper method for string truncation
Test framework uses xUnit with [Fact] attributes and Moq for mocking
BuildDetail.razor mixes UI markup with @code { } block for C# implementation
Conditional rendering uses @if/@elif/@else patterns for tab visibility
Tab switching uses StateHasChanged() to trigger UI re-render

agent_retro.what_worked_well: Clean codebase with well-documented architecture document, clear separation of concerns between UI and code blocks, established test patterns
agent_retro.challenges: No dotnet runtime available to verify tests, need to trust implementation based on code review
agent_retro.tips_for_similar_issues: Check architecture document for interface specifications before implementation, consider making private state fields accessible via reflection for testing
